title: The Atlantic — Ideas that matter
description: T660 / T73 cycle 5: theatlantic.com landing rebuilt as a Forge CmsPage. SECOND news-vertical rebuild. The decisive test of the ArticleCard dedup theory: 1-of-1 from NYT bumps to 2-of-2 if Atlantic surfaces the same pattern, which would promote ArticleCard from MED to T70 HIGH priority for the news/publication vertical.

sections.title: Ideas that matter
sections.lede: Long-form reporting on politics, culture, and the slow architecture of how we live. Independent. Subscriber-supported.

sections.title: GAP-T660-NEW3 (re-surface) — ArticleCard NOW 2-OF-2 news-vertical
body: Atlantic's homepage is, like NYT's, dominated by ArticleCards: a hero image, a kicker (issue / section), a headline, a 1-sentence dek, an author byline with link to profile, a read-time. Same pattern as cycle 4 (NYT). The ArticleCard variant now has 2-of-2 news rebuilds — bump from MED to T70 HIGH for the news/publication vertical.

Shape continues to fit: CmsSection::ArticleCard { headline, dek?, byline, dateline, thumbnail?, primary_link, kicker? }. Atlantic adds an extra signal — a 'magazine' kicker when the piece is from the print edition (vs daily web). The optional kicker field already covers that.

Going forward: a 3rd publication rebuild (Vox / Wired / a magazine) is no longer needed to validate; ArticleCard is the locked baseline for news. The next news rebuild (if owner picks one) is for SECONDARY variant discovery (live-blog, newsletter-card, comments, etc.) not ArticleCard validation.

sections.body: When the only people writing the rules are the people who profit from the loopholes, the laws stop pretending they're for everyone.

sections.title: GAP-T660-NEW6 (NEW) — NewsletterCard / SubscriptionPrompt
body: Atlantic interleaves 'Subscribe to The Daily' style newsletter-card promos between article cards every 4-6 stories. These are different from a CTA banner — they have their own headline / blurb / inline-email-form (or 'open in app' button), and they appear MID-feed rather than as a hero CTA.

Forge's existing 'banner' CmsSection handles eye-catch info banners but not the form-having + mid-feed pattern. Proposed shape: CmsSection::NewsletterCard { kicker, title, blurb, form_action: NavLink, button_label }. Email collection via the existing WriteCapability flow (T577).

Currently 1-of-2 news rebuilds (Atlantic only; NYT mostly uses static Subscribe CTAs in the header). Dedup count: MED priority. A magazine rebuild (e.g., Wired) would likely surface it again — HIGH if so.

items.value: 'The age of polite collapse' by Mira Ostrov — what it means when institutions decay without anyone breaking them.
'Eighteen months in the kitchen of an Italian widow' by Hank Salinger — a profile of food, grief, and place.
'Letters from a melting town' by Jules Brando — climate field report from the Alaskan north slope.
Five new releases reviewed; standalone essays on rereading Joan Didion at 50.

sections.text: T660 cycle 5 (2nd news rebuild) decisive: ArticleCard is NOW 2-of-2 news-vertical surfaces → T70 HIGH priority for news/publication sites. NewsletterCard surfaces 1-of-2; MED priority. Quote variant remains 6-of-6 universal across ALL marketing + news + animated rebuilds (Stripe + Linear + Vercel + NYT + Atlantic + aristidebenoist) — LOCKED as a baseline primitive.
